Next_Step_History__c (2026-02-17 to 2026-06-22); no budget field populated

items.quote: connect with SC team - fitment gaps too high, no longer responding to this RFP
items.source: Competitors__c blank, but competitive context is known from Next_Step_History__c 2026-03-12 entry
OpportunityContactRole (0 records) despite named contacts (Leslie McElhaney, Josh Ullrich, Gina Rendar) appearing in the 2026-02-26 call

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. Josh Ullrich described the current state qualitatively on the 26 Feb 2026 demo call (incidents tracked via a web-based task system that emails a fixed chain and 'disappears from the flow' if replies happen outside that system; supplier and incident performance are tracked manually in Excel scorecards), but no cost, time, or risk figures have been attached to that pain, and no ROI or savings conversation has occurred at any point in the Next Step History through 2026-06-22.

champion.narrative: No confirmed champion exists. Leslie McElhaney has been the most consistently responsive contact (she replied on 2026-06-04 confirming the RFP timeline slip and continued Zycus inclusion) but her role has been limited to RFP-logistics coordination, not internal advocacy, and there is no evidence she has championed Zycus to any unengaged senior stakeholder.

competition.narrative: No named rival vendor has surfaced for the full S2P RFP in any source (Competitors__c is blank and no vendor was named on the one recorded call). What is known: Zycus self-withdrew from an earlier, narrower Supplier Incident Management RFP on 2026-03-12 because 'fitment gaps too high,' per Grace Kim's own Next Step note, which raises an open risk that similar gaps could resurface in the broader S2P evaluation. In the meantime, the buyer's real alternative is doing nothing differently: incidents and supplier scorecards continue to run on the existing web-task system and manual Excel sheets.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and specific. Josh Ullrich described on the 26 Feb 2026 call that incidents are currently reported through a web-based task system that routes to a fixed email chain, and that any reply happening outside that system's email 'kinda disappears from the flow.' Grace Kim's Next Step notes (2026-02-26) also record the buyer's interest in dynamic, store-level incident creation from a tablet/web app and in replacing manual Excel scorecards for supplier and incident performance tracking.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process has started. The deal is still pre-RFP-release at Qualified stage; there has been no mention of MSA, SOW, procurement portal, or contracting mechanics in any Next Step entry through 2026-06-22 or on the one recorded call.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified anywhere in the record. OpportunityContactRole has zero rows on this opp. Nine contacts exist at the account level (including CSCO Chris Ward, Michael Blair, Bobby Knight, Minh Le), but none has appeared in a call, a Next Step entry, or a Task for this opportunity. The most senior attendee on the one recorded call, Gina Rendar, left after roughly 17 minutes asking to see the platform directly rather than the onboarding walkthrough, and has not reappeared in any dated activity since 26 Feb 2026 (~133 days ago).

decision_process.narrative: The buyer runs a formal RFP process via its 'Symphony' eSourcing system, with vendors down-selected post-submission for a further round of demos/presentations (stated explicitly on the 26 Feb 2026 call). Per prior sweep notes carried forward, a third-party procurement advisory firm, Maine Pointe, is formally overseeing the RFP evaluation; its scoring criteria and vendor preferences are unknown to Zycus, which limits how confidently the process can be read.

sources: Avoma call 2026-02-26 (Leslie McElhaney, Gina Rendar, Josh Ullrich)
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are still forming and will formally arrive with the S2P RFP. On the 26 Feb 2026 call, Leslie McElhaney explained the RFP will ask 'what else' each vendor offers beyond incident management, because the buying committee is weighing whether to select a true end-to-end Source-to-Pay platform versus a narrower supplier-governance point solution that would then need to integrate with a separate spend-management system. Josh Ullrich and Gina Rendar also probed weighted scorecard/KPI logic (1-to-5 rubric with weighting) as a capability they want.

items.name: Close date pushed ~6 weeks
items.summary: Close date moved from 2026-07-14 to 2026-08-27, a meaningful slip that signals delay on the buyer side or a timeline reset; no accompanying note explains the cause.

day_summary.overall: The Checkers - Rally's close date got pushed roughly six weeks, from late July to end of August. That's the only recorded activity today — no note explaining the reason, but a slip of this size usually means either a stalled internal process on their side or a re-scoping of the deal that isn't reflected yet. We're not shown as waiting on a specific deliverable, so the ball is effectively in our court to find out why and re-engage.

cro_panel.intro: Early-stage, pre-RFP-release opportunity that has cooled since a single demo in February 2026; the buyer reaffirmed continued inclusion as recently as 4 Jun 2026 but has gone quiet since, and the deal still lacks an economic buyer, a confirmed champion, or any quantified business case

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Buyer gone dark 133 days; 0 buyer touches in the 30/60/90-day windows since the 26 Feb 2026 demo. ► Re-engage all three C&R contacts directly to confirm the opportunity is still live

No economic buyer identified; MEDDPICC lists EB as 'gap'; no CPO/CFO/CIO contact appears anywhere in the record for a $125k decision. ► Ask current contacts to name and introduce the real budget owner before the RFP drops
No confirmed champion; Leslie McElhaney's role is limited to RFP-logistics coordination with no internal advocacy noted. ► Coach her into an active champion role or identify a new internal sponsor

S2P RFP repeatedly slipped; release pushed end-Apr → May → early-July per the Next Step trail, and is overdue as of 6/22/26 with no buyer update. ► Get written confirmation of a firm date or flag the 8/27 close as at risk
Related RFP loss signal; Zycus withdrew from Checkers' incident-mgmt RFP on 12 Mar 2026 citing 'fitment gaps too high,' raising doubt on fit for the parallel S2P effort

One-way rep chasing; six straight Next-Step entries (3/17, 3/30, 4/13, 5/26, 6/4, 6/22) show the rep repeatedly following up with no logged buyer reply. ► Escalate via a warmer or higher-level contact since this channel isn't generating response

Deal not confirmed dead; Leslie's 6/4/26 response confirmed Checkers & Rally's 'will still be included' when the S2P RFP releases, keeping the door open despite the silence

fit.ai_fit_signal: The buyer has not voiced explicit enthusiasm or resistance toward AI capabilities; engagement with the AI-specific demo segments was passive rather than probing.
fit.expectations_fit: The buyer wants a blend of near-term supplier governance/incident management and a longer-term full Source-to-Pay suite; the 26 Feb 2026 demo covered only the former, leaving the latter unvalidated against Zycus.
fit.positioning_strength: Zycus proactively wove AI messaging throughout the 26 Feb 2026 demo (Merlin agentic AI framing, the Merlin supplier-health assistant, an AI-driven smart-onboarding/OCR extraction app, and the Questgenie AI question-builder for scorecards) e

deal_movement.summary: The deal has moved through a demo and an RFP-receipt milestone in Feb–Mar 2026, then stalled in a repeating cycle of buyer-side RFP delays (end of April → May → start of Q3 → first week of July) with no stage advancement since creation on 2026-02-11.

forecast_read.math: Created 2026-02-11 (~148 days ago). Days to close: 49 (close 2026-08-27). Time in Qualified stage: ~148 days with no stage advancement since creation. Last confirmed buyer reply: 2026-06-04 (35 days ago). Last rep outreach: 2026-06-22 (17 days ago, unanswered).
forecast_read.reason: Best Case is not supported by the evidence: the deal is Qualified stage, still pre-RFP-release (the full S2P RFP itself has not yet reached Zycus after four stated slips), single-threaded to two mid-level contacts, with no economic buyer, no budget conversation, and a close date (27 Aug 2026, 49 days out) that assumes RFP issuance, response, evaluation, down-select and contracting can all complete in under seven weeks.

critical_signals.text: Zycus pulled itself out of the buyer's first, narrower RFP on 12 Mar 2026 because of fitment gaps, a self-inflicted vulnerability that could resurface once the fuller evaluation arrives.

The only call to date, on 26 Feb 2026, was deliberately narrowed to incident management after the buyer's most senior attendee said she didn't want the general walkthrough and left the call early asking to be looped in directly instead.

No economic buyer or decision maker has engaged in nearly five months; the deal still runs through two mid-level contacts, one of whom (Leslie McElhaney) is coordinating logistics rather than championing Zycus internally.

The buyer's own RFP timeline has now slipped past 'end of April,' 'May,' 'start of Q3,' and most recently 'first week of July' without release, and the account has gone quiet since confirming that last slip on 4 Jun 2026.

items: One buyer meeting on record since 2026-02-26 (~133 days ago)
confidence_signals.summary: The account confirmed continued inclusion as recently as 2026-06-04 (35 days ago), but has not replied to the rep's 2026-06-22 follow-up (17 days unanswered as of this sweep); combined with a single demo in over four months, the deal is cooling rather than dead.

north_star_verdict.math: 146 days at Qualified since creation (11 Feb 2026), no stage advance. Close date has slipped twice: 25 May 2026 -> 14 Jul 2026 (+50 days, moved 11 May 2026) -> 27 Aug 2026 (+44 days, moved 15 Jun 2026), a combined 94-day slip from the earliest recorded target. Last confirmed two-way buyer exchange was 4 Jun 2026 (33 days ago); the most recent rep follow-up (22 Jun 2026, 15 days ago) awaiting the 'first week of July' RFP release has gone unanswered. 51 days remain to the current 27 Aug close date, and the RFP that would trigger real evaluation has still not been confirmed released.
evidence: Next_Step_History__c 2026-06-04: 'Leslie response - S2P release pushed to beginning of Q3, we will still be included'
Next_Step_History__c 2026-06-22: 'awaiting RFP release first week of July' (no further update logged as of 7 Jul 2026)
OpportunityFieldHistory: StageName Initial Interest -> Qualified same day as creation (11 Feb 2026), no subsequent stage movement
north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is single-threaded to two operational Checkers contacts awaiting a Source-to-Pay RFP that has slipped three times and is now overdue past its last-stated window, with no economic buyer or champion in place.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (RFP not yet confirmed released, 0 contact roles, no EB or champion identified; Best Case is not supportable at Qualified stage 51 days out)

flags: Re-engage the buyer directly by phone (not email) — the deal has been silent for 129 days and the RFP is a week overdue; if no reply in 5 business days, disqualify or move to nurture.

Single-threaded to two operational contacts (Josh Ullrich, Leslie McElhaney) plus once-mentioned Gina Rendar four-plus months after the last meeting; add Contact Roles and open a path to Chris Ward (CSCO), Michael Blair (Strategy Officer), or Minh Le before the S2P RFP lands.

Quantify the business case — the buyer described operational pain (lost incident emails, manual Excel tracking) but never stated the cost, FTE burden, or savings; build the ROI to justify the $125k investment.

Zycus self-withdrew from the SIM RFP on 12 Mar 2026 citing fitment gaps; pre-brief Josh or Leslie on how the full-suite scope resolves those gaps so Zycus isn't pre-judged as a fit risk again in the S2P round.

A third-party advisory firm (Maine Pointe) is running a structured cost/timeline scoring process; get the actual evaluation criteria directly from Josh or Leslie so Zycus's S2P submission is built to the real rubric, not written blind after release.

Confirm or build a real champion: Leslie McElhaney has only played an RFP-coordination role to date; test her willingness to advocate internally, or identify an alternate advocate given Gina Rendar's early exit from the one demo held.
Re-engage now that the 'first week of July' release target has passed without confirmation, referencing the pattern of four prior slips, to avoid losing visibility before the RFP even reaches Zycus.
best_practice_check.summary: The highest-leverage moves are multi-threading to a genuine economic buyer and re-establishing a live response after 35 days of buyer silence, ahead of the still-unreleased S2P RFP.

customer_preference.evidence: Leslie McElhaney confirmed on 2026-06-04 that Zycus 'will still be included' in the S2P RFP, but no explicit preference for Zycus over any alternative has been voiced, and Zycus already self-withdrew from a related RFP on 2026-03-12 over fitment concerns.

competitive_position.summary: No named vendor competitor has surfaced in any source (Competitors__c is blank, no Avoma call or Next_Step mentions a rival by name). The only competitive signal is Checkers' own RFP-process field noting a 'competitive vendor landscape with specialized point solutions' evaluated under strict cost/timeline criteria with third-party advisor Maine Pointe overseeing the process (SF Why_not_Zycus__c). Zycus itself self-eliminated from the first-phase Supplier Incident Management RFP on 12 Mar 2026, citing 'fitment gaps too high' (Next_Step__c), while remaining engaged for the larger Source-to-Pay suite RFP expected in Q3 2026. The strongest current threat is Zycus's own unresolved fit gap from the SIM round, plus the buyer's status-quo manual/Excel process, which could persist as a 'do nothing' outcome if the S2P RFP keeps slipping.

competitors.name: Status quo (manual Excel/email process)
competitors.quote: Currently, it's ... like a web based ... task management system. So they'll go in there, they'll create their ticket, and then it creates an email that goes to a predefined chain of people ... if the communication goes off of that specific system generated email, it kinda disappears from the flow.
competitors.how_we_win: Keep the pain concrete (lost communications, manual Excel scorecards) tied to the dynamic routing and scorecard-building capability shown in the demo, and press for a decision date so the status quo doesn't simply persist by default while the RFP keeps slipping.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early-stage, pre-RFP-release opportunity that has cooled since a single demo in February 2026; the buyer reaffirmed continued inclusion as recently as 4 Jun 2026 but has gone quiet since, and the deal still lacks an economic buyer, a confirmed champion, or any quantified business case.

deal_risk.text: Close date of 27 Aug 2026 (49 days from today) is not credible for a Qualified-stage deal that hasn't yet received the RFP it's waiting on, let alone submitted, been shortlisted, or engaged an economic buyer.

A third-party procurement advisory firm, Maine Pointe, is understood to be overseeing the RFP evaluation; its scoring weightings and any vendor preferences are unknown to Zycus, adding evaluation risk outside Zycus's control.

win_position.text: Buyer confirmed continued inclusion — Leslie McElhaney told Grace Kim on 4 Jun 2026 that the S2P RFP release had been pushed to the start of Q3 but that Zycus 'will still be included,' the most recent direct signal Zycus hasn't been dropped.

No economic buyer or decision maker identified — none of the nine known Checkers & Rally's contacts, including CSCO Chris Ward, CFO Michael Blair, and CIO Minh Le, has a logged contact role or any recorded involvement on this opportunity.

Zycus already withdrew once — the 12 Mar 2026 Next Step note records Zycus stepping back from the Supplier Incident Management RFP for 'fitment gaps too high,' an unresolved risk for the fuller S2P evaluation.

Single-threaded and cooling — the only senior attendee, Gina Rendar, left the 26 Feb 2026 demo early wanting a more direct look and hasn't reappeared since; Leslie McElhaney and Josh Ullrich remain the sole active contacts.

deal_momentum.text: RFP release has slipped at least four times — from end of April (30 Mar note), to May (12/17 Mar notes), to start of Q3 (4 Jun note), to 'first week of July' (22 Jun note), and as of 9 Jul 2026 that date has passed with no release or reply.

No buyer-initiated contact in 35 days — the last buyer reply was Leslie McElhaney's 4 Jun 2026 message; Grace Kim's 22 Jun 2026 follow-up to all three known contacts has gone unanswered for 17 days as of today.

Only one buyer meeting in over four months — the sole demo was held 26 Feb 2026 (~133 days ago) and was deliberately narrowed to incident management; the promised full-suite follow-up demo for Gina Rendar was never scheduled.
The relationship hasn't gone fully silent — Leslie McElhaney's 4 Jun 2026 message shows the account is still tracking Zycus internally even as its own RFP timeline slips.

customer_commitment.text: No commercial, budget, or approval-process conversation has occurred at any point in the Next Step History (2026-02-17 through 2026-06-22), consistent with a pre-RFP, pre-qualification deal.
The one clear buyer ask — Gina Rendar's request for a prerecorded full end-to-end S2P suite video, made 26 Feb 2026 — has no confirmed delivery on record over four months later.

items.quote: That would be wonderful if you could send that ... that video would be wonderful ... what your full end to end system looks like.
items.requirement: Send pre-recorded demo video of full end-to-end S2P suite to Leslie McElhaney and Gina Rendar

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus proactively wove AI messaging throughout the 26 Feb 2026 demo (Merlin agentic AI framing, the Merlin supplier-health assistant, an AI-driven smart-onboarding/OCR extraction app, and the Questgenie AI question-builder for scorecards) even though the buyer did not ask AI-specific questions.

items.quote: we are prepping the next phase is to look at a full spend management suite ... source to pay, procure to pay
Completely dynamic. So one thing that Zycus does ... is that we do provide dynamic workflows and routing logic
items.criterion: Dynamic, store-level incident creation and routing

items.quote: Taking those KPIs, translating them ... into a scorecard of that type, is that something that you have the option to build as well?
items.criterion: Weighted KPI/scorecard rubric (1-5 scale) for supplier performance

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The buyer wants a blend of near-term supplier governance/incident management and a longer-term full Source-to-Pay suite; the 26 Feb 2026 demo covered only the former, leaving the latter unvalidated against Zycus.

value.name: Status quo (manual Excel/email process)
value.quote: Currently, it's ... like a web based ... task management system. So they'll go in there, they'll create their ticket, and then it creates an email that goes to a predefined chain of people ... if the communication goes off of that specific system generated email, it kinda disappears from the flow.
value.how_we_win: Keep the pain concrete (lost communications, manual Excel scorecards) tied to the dynamic routing and scorecard-building capability shown in the demo, and press for a decision date so the status quo doesn't simply persist by default while the RFP keeps slipping.
